expectations.text: all-required-files-created: outputs contains main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/fetcher.py, README.md
expectations.evidence: All 5 required files present under projects/data_fetcher/
Line '-r projects/data_fetcher/requirements.txt' found in root requirements.txt

expectations.text: main-py-is-thin: main.py is 15 lines or fewer and contains no business logic
expectations.evidence: main.py is 24 lines and contains a print() call. Architecture is also more complex: instantiates FetcherConfig + Fetcher separately rather than a single class.

expectations.text: main-py-imports-from-libs: main.py imports the Fetcher class from libs
class-has-type-hints: Fetcher class has typed __init__ parameters and typed method return values
expectations.evidence: Full type hints present, including parameterized generics like dict[str, str] | None

expectations.text: no-optional-syntax: libs/fetcher.py does not use Optional[] syntax
expectations.evidence: Uses 'str | None' union syntax. No Optional imports found.

expectations.text: readme-has-real-content: README.md contains usage and project structure sections
expectations.evidence: 78-line README with extensive documentation: usage, argument table, error conditions, class reference

notes: Baseline produced FULL IMPLEMENTATION in libs/fetcher.py (urllib HTTP calls, URL builder, __post_init__ validation) instead of a skeleton. This is the critical failure vs with-skill but isn't captured by current assertions — need a 'methods-are-skeleton' assertion.
